sections.title: 02a — Phase inputs
sections.content:
### Commodity Functional Promise (primary search driver)

> **Deliver operational grid-connected electrical energy storage assets as turnkey capital projects with single-source engineering, procurement, construction, and grid-code certification**

### FP Extensions (secondary search drivers)

- **Axis 1 — Storage medium neutrality:** Flow-battery EPC, flywheel/CAES EPC, thermal-to-power storage EPC, hybrid H2-BESS EPC
- **Axis 2 — Grid-connected capital-asset EPC methodology:** Large-scale PV EPC, HPC charging hub EPC, PV+Wind hybrid EPC, industrial microgrid EPC, behind-the-meter electrification EPC

### UNSPSC / commodity context

No 8-digit UNSPSC ≥80% confidence. Custom group: **"Battery Energy Storage System EPC Services"**.

### Excluded markets (current business)

| Excluded | Reason |
|---|---|
| Residential rooftop PV (Mein Solar incumbent) | Below EPC scale threshold (<500 kW); current market |
| Small commercial PV <500 kW | Current market; below BESS EPC minimum scale |
| Pure-cell manufacturing (26111711 category) | Up-stream component, not customer |

---

sections.title: 02a — Candidate NAICS discovery (all candidates, no cap)
sections.content:

| 2 | 221114 | Solar Electric Power Generation | Utilities | direct | established | primary_fp + extension |

| 8 | 457120 | Other Gasoline Stations and Fuel Dealers (incl. EV charging retail) | Retail | adjacent | emerging | fp_extension |

**Total: 20 candidates.** Exceeds 20-candidate threshold → **Tiered Discovery triggered** for Phase 02b.

sections.title: 02b — Architecture-distance ranking (product-specific)
sections.content:
### Product identity for scoring

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Product | EPC für Großbatteriespeicher |
| Technology class | Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC |
| Product FP | Deliver grid-compliant, dispatchable multi-MWh electrical energy storage as a turnkey capital asset with single-source responsibility |
| Mechanism | Turnkey systems integration of containerized Li-ion BESS + bidirectional PCS + MV coupling + EMS + civil, single-source EPC contract |
| Key specs | 4.5 MW / 9 MWh reference, ≥500 kW / ≥1 MWh min, LFP, 20ft/40ft ISO HC, VDE-AR-N 4110, FGW TR8, €250–400/kWh installed |
| Scoring type | **Capital equipment (L5–L6)** — project-business delivery of a commissioned L0 asset |

### Scoring criteria (Universal + Capital-equipment-specific)

- **U1** Operating environment similarity (outdoor containerized, MV-connected industrial site)
- **U2** Regulatory regime distance (VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 transferability)
- **U3** Customer type similarity (IPP / asset owner / C&I offtaker)
- **U4** Integration architecture (MV grid-coupled capital asset)
- **U5** Price point alignment (€250–400/kWh, multi-MWh project, 3–5yr payback)
- **C1** Installation requirements match (MV feeder, 40–80 m²/MWh, civil works, Baugenehmigung)
- **C2** Operator skill transferability (DSO interface, EMS dispatch, SCADA)
- **C3** Maintenance infrastructure match (LFP O&M, PCS service, annual cert audits)
- **C4** Reimbursement/funding model (KfW/innovation tenders, revenue stack: peak shaving / §19 StromNEV / EPEX / FCR-aFRR)

### Tier 1 — Quick screen (all 20 candidates)

Cut threshold: ≥8 distance AND `already_uses_technology = false`. All candidates survived Tier 1 except #18 (Cement) and #20 (Wireless Telecom cell towers — typical BESS size <500 kW, below minimum project scale).

### Tier 2 — Full assessment (18 candidates scored on 9 criteria)

Sorted by composite distance ascending.

| Rank | NAICS | Title | Distance | Already uses | Priority | U1 | U2 | U3 | U4 | U5 | C1 | C2 | C3 | C4 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 221118 | Other Electric Power Generation | **1** | TRUE | HIGH | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 2 |
| 2 | 221114 | Solar Electric Power Generation | **2** | TRUE | HIGH | 2 | 1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 2 | 1 | 2 | 2 |
| 3 | 221115 | Wind Electric Power Generation | **3** | TRUE | HIGH | 3 | 1 | 3 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 2 | 3 | 2 |
| 4 | 531120 | Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings | **3** | TRUE | HIGH | 2 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 4 | 3 | 2 | 3 | 4 |
| 5 | 457120 | Other Gasoline Stations (EV charging) | **3** | FALSE | HIGH | 3 | 2 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 4 |
| 6 | 221122 | Electric Power Distribution | **4** | TRUE | HIGH | 2 | 2 | 5 | 2 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 3 | 5 |
| 7 | 518210 | Computing Infrastructure / Data Centers | **4** | TRUE | HIGH | 3 | 2 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 3 | 4 | 4 | 4 |
| 8 | 493120 | Refrigerated Warehousing and Storage | **4** | FALSE | MEDIUM | 3 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 4 | 4 | 3 | 4 | 5 |
| 9 | 325120 | Industrial Gas Mfg (electrolyzers / H2) | **5** | FALSE | MEDIUM | 4 | 2 | 5 | 4 | 5 | 5 | 4 | 5 | 6 |
| 10 | 488310 | Port and Harbor Operations | **5** | FALSE | MEDIUM | 6 | 3 | 5 | 4 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 6 |
| 11 | 221121 | Electric Bulk Power Transmission | **6** | TRUE | MEDIUM | 3 | 6 | 7 | 3 | 7 | 6 | 4 | 4 | 6 |
| 12 | 334413 | Semiconductor Manufacturing | **6** | FALSE | MEDIUM | 5 | 3 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 7 |
| 13 | 488190 | Air Transportation Support (airports) | **6** | FALSE | LOW | 6 | 4 | 6 | 5 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 7 |
| 14 | 221320 | Sewage Treatment Facilities | **6** | FALSE | LOW | 4 | 3 | 7 | 4 | 6 | 6 | 5 | 5 | 8 |
| 15 | 111419 | Other Food Crops Grown Under Cover | **7** | FALSE | LOW | 5 | 3 | 7 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 6 | 7 | 8 |
| 16 | 331110 | Iron and Steel Mills | **7** | FALSE | LOW | 6 | 4 | 7 | 6 | 7 | 7 | 6 | 6 | 8 |
| 17 | 331313 | Alumina Refining / Primary Aluminum | **7** | FALSE | LOW | 6 | 4 | 8 | 6 | 8 | 7 | 7 | 6 | 8 |
| 18 | 928110 | National Security (military bases) | **7** | FALSE | LOW | 5 | 7 | 8 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 6 | 7 | 9 |

### Tier 1 cuts (excluded before full scoring)

| NAICS | Title | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| 327310 | Cement Manufacturing | Distance ≥8, not_yet adoption; kiln electrification still pre-pilot |
| 517312 | Wireless Telecommunications Carriers | Typical tower BESS <100 kWh, below ≥1 MWh minimum EPC scale (constraint P2) |

### Selected markets (top 15 by default `max_markets=15`)

Ranks 1–15 above proceed as `MarketCandidate` objects. The remaining 3 (ranks 16–18) are marked `status="cut_by_max_markets"` but retained with distance scores for Phase 02c selection by `max_vns`.

---

sections.title: 02b — Ranked output with rationale
sections.content:
### Rank 1 — NAICS 221118 Other Electric Power Generation · distance **1** · HIGH

**Rationale:** Literally the standard NAICS for standalone BESS IPPs in the US/EU. Customer is an asset owner / IPP that exists to operate grid-connected storage. Every Mein Solar differentiator (single-source EPC, VDE-AR-N 4110, FGW TR8) is exactly what this buyer needs. Only friction: pure-play BESS IPPs often have in-house EPC talent, so competition is intense against tier-1 integrators (Fluence, Tesla, Sungrow).
**Already uses technology:** TRUE
**Current alternatives hired:** In-house EPC teams, Fluence, Tesla Megapack turnkey, Sungrow PowerTitan integrators, Wärtsilä

### Rank 2 — NAICS 221114 Solar Electric Power Generation · distance **2** · HIGH

**Rationale:** PV+BESS co-location is industry-standard. Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency is a direct differentiator here: one grid connection, one certification dossier, one EMS, one warranty. Same VDE-AR-N 4110, same customer persona (Solarpark developers, IPPs, community-solar co-ops), same DSO interface. Solar developers already retrofit storage onto operational PV parks.
**Already uses technology:** TRUE
**Current alternatives hired:** In-house, specialized BESS integrators retrofitting onto PV sites, Enerparc/BayWa r.e., Goldbeck Solar

### Rank 3 — NAICS 221115 Wind Electric Power Generation · distance **3** · HIGH

**Rationale:** Wind parks increasingly co-deploy BESS for curtailment capture and ancillary-service prequal. Same grid code (VDE-AR-N 4110 / 4120 for HV), same certification path. Customer persona shifts to wind developers (Enercon, WPD, Energiequelle) — adjacent not identical. Mein Solar lacks wind-specific site heritage but the EPC methodology translates cleanly.
**Already uses technology:** TRUE
**Current alternatives hired:** Wind OEM storage offerings (Vestas, Siemens Gamesa), pure-play BESS integrators

### Rank 4 — NAICS 531120 Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings · distance **3** · HIGH

**Rationale:** C&I landlords (logistics parks, office campuses, retail centers) deploying behind-the-meter BESS for peak-shaving Netzentgelte reduction and tenant resilience. Same MV grid interface, same Baugenehmigung process, same €/kWh budgets. Perfect match for Mein Solar's "single-source EPC" promise because landlords hate multi-vendor coordination.
**Already uses technology:** TRUE (growing rapidly 2025–2027)
**Current alternatives hired:** Voltfang, Tesvolt, Commeo turnkey units, in-house facilities management

### Rank 5 — NAICS 457120 Other Gasoline Stations (incl. EV charging retail) · distance **3** · HIGH

**Rationale:** **Explicit Mein Solar differentiator** — "EV-Charging Buffer Configuration" feature. HPC hubs (Ionity, EnBW mobility+, Fastned, Aral pulse) face weak rural MV grids; a BESS buffer lets the operator install 350 kW × 6+ dispensers on a 400 kVA feeder. Same grid code, same single-source contract appeal. FP extension axis: "HPC charging park EPC".
**Already uses technology:** FALSE (emerging — 2024–2026 inflection)
**Current alternatives hired:** Kostad, ads-tec Energy, Alpitronic integrated BESS+HPC, DIY split-contract

### Rank 6 — NAICS 221122 Electric Power Distribution · distance **4** · HIGH

**Rationale:** DSO-owned "Netzbooster" storage for grid constraint relief, voltage support, and deferred substation upgrade. Customer is utility (Bayernwerk, Netze BW, Stromnetz Hamburg, Westnetz) — longer procurement cycles, EU-tender rules, stricter interoperability (IEC 61850). Same MV scale as Mein Solar reference. Utility segment is slower but contracts are larger and predictable.
**Already uses technology:** TRUE
**Current alternatives hired:** ABB, Siemens Grid Integration, Hitachi Energy, Nidec ASI

### Rank 7 — NAICS 518210 Computing Infrastructure Providers (Data Centers) · distance **4** · HIGH

**Rationale:** Hyperscalers and colocation operators (NTT, Digital Realty, Vantage, Equinix, CyrusOne, STACK) replacing lead-acid UPS with Li-ion BESS + grid-services revenue stacking. Same MV scale, same VDE-AR-N 4110 regime, but the operational expectation is mission-critical (99.999% availability) and fire-protection scrutiny is extreme. Strong growth vector: DE data-center capacity doubling 2025–2030.
**Already uses technology:** TRUE (growing — hybrid UPS/BESS adoption inflecting)
**Current alternatives hired:** Schneider Electric Galaxy VXL, Vertiv, Eaton, Piller

### Rank 8 — NAICS 493120 Refrigerated Warehousing and Storage · distance **4** · MEDIUM

**Rationale:** Cold storage (Nordfrost, Kühne+Nagel cold chain, Frigoscandia) carries 24/7 high-kW refrigeration load; BESS enables peak-shaving and tariff arbitrage, plus resilience against spoilage during outages. Same MV interface, same C&I grid code, same site type as industrial landlord. Customer awareness is low — education-led sale.
**Already uses technology:** FALSE (emerging)
**Current alternatives hired:** Diesel gensets, utility tariff negotiation, no storage

### Rank 9 — NAICS 325120 Industrial Gas Manufacturing (electrolyzer/H2 sites) · distance **5** · MEDIUM

**Rationale:** Green-hydrogen projects (Linde, Air Liquide, Iberdrola, RWE green-H2) pair electrolyzers with BESS for curtailment capture, grid-forming support, and ramp-rate smoothing. Same grid code, same MV coupling, but the EMS must be co-optimized with the electrolyzer — different control logic. Mein Solar's integration discipline transfers; the hydrogen control layer is new skill.
**Already uses technology:** FALSE (emerging 2025–2028)
**Current alternatives hired:** Electrolyzer OEM turnkey packages (Nel, Sunfire, Siemens Energy), split-contract with BESS integrator

### Rank 10 — NAICS 488310 Port and Harbor Operations · distance **5** · MEDIUM

**Rationale:** Port electrification (shore power / OPS, reach-stacker charging, ferry charging, crane smoothing). Customers are port authorities (HHLA Hamburg, BLG Bremerhaven, Duisport). Environment is salt-air coastal (IP upgrade required), grid scale matches Mein Solar reference. Public procurement + EU/German port-modernization subsidies. Different vertical but methodology transfers.
**Already uses technology:** FALSE (emerging — shore power mandate 2030)
**Current alternatives hired:** Siemens, ABB, Wärtsilä port-solutions, in-port-authority engineering

### Rank 11 — NAICS 221121 Electric Bulk Power Transmission and Control · distance **6** · MEDIUM

**Rationale:** TSO-scale grid-forming storage (50 Hertz, TenneT, Amprion, TransnetBW). Asset scale is typically 100–500 MW — **an order of magnitude above Mein Solar's 4.5 MW reference**. Different grid code (VDE-AR-N 4120 HV), different certification (Type A HV plant cert), different customer (regulated TSO, EU-tender-driven). Reachable only via consortium bid or subcontractor role.
**Already uses technology:** TRUE
**Current alternatives hired:** Hitachi Energy, Siemens Energy, GE Vernova, Mitsubishi Electric

### Rank 12 — NAICS 334413 Semiconductor Manufacturing · distance **6** · MEDIUM

**Rationale:** Fabs (Infineon Dresden, GlobalFoundries, Bosch SiC, Intel Magdeburg-announced) buy power-quality + ride-through solutions. BESS supplements dynamic UPS / flywheels for sag ride-through (200 ms events). Fab customer has different spec language (ITIC curve, SEMI F47), custom engineering per site. Entry is hard without fab reference.
**Already uses technology:** FALSE (partial — STATCOM + flywheel dominant; BESS emerging)
**Current alternatives hired:** ABB PCS100, Piller UNIBLOCK, static VAR compensators, bespoke UPS

### Rank 13 — NAICS 488190 Other Support Activities for Air Transportation · distance **6** · LOW

**Rationale:** Airport GSE charging, eTaxi tractor charging, eVTOL vertiport readiness (Frankfurt, Munich). Infrastructure-ownership split between airport operator and handling agents complicates sale. Environment is apron-adjacent, safety-critical for aviation-side clearance. Emerging market with long decision cycles.
**Already uses technology:** FALSE (emerging)
**Current alternatives hired:** OEM GSE charging packages (ITW GSE, TLD), airport-utility direct grid upgrade

### Rank 14 — NAICS 221320 Sewage Treatment Facilities · distance **6** · LOW

**Rationale:** Municipal WWTPs with peak pumping loads + co-location with biogas CHP create storage opportunity (FCR participation, demand charge reduction, CHP firming). Customer is municipality — slow procurement, GWB-regulated tenders, no revenue-stacking culture. Niche adjacent to Mein Solar's core.
**Already uses technology:** FALSE
**Current alternatives hired:** Diesel backup, CHP-only, utility tariff

### Rank 15 — NAICS 111419 Other Food Crops Grown Under Cover (greenhouses) · distance **7** · LOW

**Rationale:** Commercial greenhouses (tomato, herb, cannabis-adjacent Dutch model) with grow-light + heat-pump loads. Electrification of greenhouse heating shifts the demand profile to peaks that BESS can smooth. Customer persona is agricultural (not Mein Solar's traditional C&I), different sales channel, different valuation metrics. Rare fit for demonstration projects only.
**Already uses technology:** FALSE
**Current alternatives hired:** Gas CHP with heat/power output, no storage

### Excluded (Tier 2 ranks 16–18 — distance ≥7, LOW priority)

| NAICS | Title | Distance | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| 331110 | Iron and Steel Mills | 7 | Arc-furnace flicker mitigation scale is 50–200 MW, ≥10× Mein Solar reference |
| 331313 | Alumina Refining / Primary Aluminum | 7 | Baseload smelter, not peak-shaveable; non-existent revenue stack |
| 928110 | National Security (military bases) | 7 | Defense-procurement cleared-prime requirement; EU-content mandate; no Mein Solar credential |

### Excluded (Tier 1 cuts)

| NAICS | Title | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| 327310 | Cement Manufacturing | Kiln electrification pre-pilot; no revenue-stack path |
| 517312 | Wireless Telecommunications Carriers | Typical site <100 kWh — below P2 minimum EPC scale |

### Market Priority Distribution

| Priority | Count | Markets |
|---|---|---|
| HIGH | 7 | 221118, 221114, 221115, 531120, 457120, 221122, 518210 |
| MEDIUM | 5 | 493120, 325120, 488310, 221121, 334413 |
| LOW | 3 | 488190, 221320, 111419 |

---

sections.title: 02b — Phase summary
sections.content:
- **Total NAICS discovered (02a):** 20
- **Tiered discovery used:** YES (>20 threshold crossed at the edge; full tier 1 applied)
- **Tier 1 cuts:** 2 (327310, 517312)
- **Tier 2 scored:** 18
- **Selected for Phase 02c:** 15 (ranks 1–15)
- **Top-priority cluster:** Utilities (221114/115/118/122) + C&I (531120/518210) + EV charging (457120) — 7 HIGH-priority markets

**Handoff:** Phase 02c (VN construction per NAICS) is out of scope for this component invocation. Separate VN agents will consume the selected 15 markets and build Value Networks for each.

---

rows.Value: Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC
Deliver grid-compliant, dispatchable multi-MWh electrical energy storage as a turnkey capital asset with single-source responsibility
Turnkey systems integration of containerized Li-ion BESS + bidirectional PCS + MV coupling + EMS + civil, single-source EPC contract
4.5 MW / 9 MWh reference, ≥500 kW / ≥1 MWh min, LFP, 20ft/40ft ISO HC, VDE-AR-N 4110, FGW TR8, €250–400/kWh installed
**Capital equipment (L5–L6)** — project-business delivery of a commissioned L0 asset

Cement Manufacturing

entities.technology_class: Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC
entities.commodity_functional_promise: Deliver operational grid-connected electrical energy storage assets as turnkey capital projects with single-source engineering, procurement, construction, and grid-code certification
fp_extensions: Storage medium neutrality — flow batteries, flywheels/CAES, thermal-to-power, hybrid H2-BESS
Grid-connected capital-asset EPC methodology — PV EPC, HPC charging hubs, PV+Wind hybrids, industrial microgrids, behind-the-meter electrification
entities.unspsc_context: custom_product_group: Battery Energy Storage System EPC Services (no UNSPSC ≥80% confidence)
excluded_markets: Residential rooftop PV (Mein Solar incumbent, below EPC scale)
Small commercial PV <500 kW (current market, below BESS EPC minimum)
Pure cell manufacturing 26111711 (upstream component, not customer)
327310 Cement Manufacturing (Tier-1 cut — kiln electrification pre-pilot)
517312 Wireless Telecommunications Carriers (Tier-1 cut — typical site <100 kWh, below P2 minimum)

markets.label: Market
markets.architecture_rationale: Literal standard NAICS for standalone BESS IPPs. Customer exists to operate grid-connected storage. Every Mein Solar differentiator (single-source EPC, VDE-AR-N 4110, FGW TR8) maps directly. Only friction: pure-play BESS IPPs often have in-house EPC talent.

markets.label: Market
markets.architecture_rationale: PV+BESS co-location is industry-standard. Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency is a direct structural differentiator here — one grid connection, one certification dossier, one EMS, one warranty wrapper. Same VDE-AR-N 4110, same customer persona (Solarpark developers), same DSO interface.

markets.label: Market
markets.architecture_rationale: Wind parks increasingly co-deploy BESS for curtailment capture and ancillary-service prequal. Same grid code, same certification path. Customer persona shifts to wind developers (Enercon, WPD, Energiequelle) — adjacent not identical. Mein Solar lacks wind-specific site heritage but EPC methodology translates cleanly.

markets.label: Market
markets.architecture_rationale: C&I landlords (logistics parks, office campuses, retail centers) deploying behind-the-meter BESS for peak-shaving Netzentgelte reduction and tenant resilience. Same MV interface, same Baugenehmigung process. Perfect match for single-source-EPC promise because landlords hate multi-vendor coordination.

markets.label: Market
markets.naics_title: Other Gasoline Stations and Fuel Dealers (incl. EV charging retail)
markets.market_name: HPC Charging Hubs with BESS Buffer
markets.architecture_rationale: Explicit Mein Solar differentiator (EV-Charging Buffer Configuration feature). HPC hubs (Ionity, EnBW mobility+, Fastned, Aral pulse) face weak rural MV grids; a BESS buffer enables 350 kW × 6+ dispensers on a 400 kVA feeder. FP-extension axis: HPC charging park EPC. Whitespace opportunity with rapidly inflecting adoption.

markets.label: Market
markets.architecture_rationale: DSO-owned storage for grid constraint relief, voltage support, and deferred substation upgrade. Customer is regulated utility (Bayernwerk, Netze BW, Stromnetz Hamburg) — longer procurement cycles, EU-tender rules, stricter interoperability (IEC 61850). Same MV scale as Mein Solar reference; utility segment is slower but contracts are larger and predictable.

markets.label: Market
markets.naics_title: Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services
markets.architecture_rationale: Hyperscalers and colocation operators (NTT, Digital Realty, Vantage, Equinix, CyrusOne, STACK) replacing lead-acid UPS with Li-ion BESS and stacking grid-services revenue. Same MV scale, same VDE-AR-N 4110 regime, but mission-critical availability (99.999%) and extreme fire-protection scrutiny. Strong growth — DE data-center capacity doubling 2025–2030.

markets.label: Market
markets.architecture_rationale: Cold storage (Nordfrost, Kühne+Nagel, Frigoscandia) carries 24/7 high-kW refrigeration load. BESS enables peak-shaving and tariff arbitrage plus spoilage-outage resilience. Same MV interface, same C&I grid code, same site type as industrial landlord. Customer awareness low — education-led sale.

markets.label: Market
markets.market_name: Green-H₂ Electrolyzer Sites with BESS Firming
markets.architecture_rationale: Green-hydrogen projects (Linde, Air Liquide, Iberdrola, RWE green-H2) pair electrolyzers with BESS for curtailment capture, grid-forming support, and ramp-rate smoothing. Same grid code, same MV coupling, but EMS must be co-optimized with the electrolyzer stack — new control logic. Mein Solar's integration discipline transfers; H2-control layer is new skill.
current_alternatives_hired: Electrolyzer OEM turnkey (Nel, Sunfire, Siemens Energy)

markets.label: Market
markets.market_name: Port Electrification — Shore Power, Crane, Ferry Charging
markets.architecture_rationale: Port electrification: shore power/OPS (2030 EU mandate), reach-stacker charging, ferry charging, crane smoothing. Customers are port authorities (HHLA Hamburg, BLG Bremerhaven, Duisport). Salt-air coastal environment requires IP upgrade. Grid scale matches Mein Solar reference. Public procurement + EU port-modernization subsidies.

markets.label: Market
markets.naics_title: Electric Bulk Power Transmission and Control
markets.architecture_rationale: TSO-scale storage (50 Hertz, TenneT, Amprion, TransnetBW) at 100–500 MW — an order of magnitude above Mein Solar's 4.5 MW reference. Different grid code (VDE-AR-N 4120 HV), different certification (Type A HV plant cert), EU-tender-driven. Reachable only via consortium bid or subcontractor role.

markets.label: Market
markets.architecture_rationale: Fabs (Infineon Dresden, GlobalFoundries, Bosch SiC, Intel Magdeburg) buy power-quality + ride-through solutions. BESS supplements dynamic UPS/flywheels for sag ride-through (200 ms events). Fab spec language (ITIC curve, SEMI F47) is bespoke; custom engineering per site. Entry difficult without fab reference.

markets.label: Market
markets.naics_title: Other Support Activities for Air Transportation
markets.market_name: Airport Electrification — GSE, eTaxi, eVTOL Readiness
markets.architecture_rationale: Airport GSE charging, eTaxi tractor charging, eVTOL vertiport readiness. Ownership split between airport operator and handling agents complicates sale. Apron-adjacent environment is safety-critical with aviation-side clearance requirements. Long decision cycles.

markets.label: Market
markets.architecture_rationale: Municipal WWTPs with peak pumping loads + co-location with biogas CHP create FCR participation and demand charge reduction opportunity. Customer is municipality — slow procurement, GWB-regulated tenders, no revenue-stacking culture. Niche adjacent to Mein Solar's core.

markets.label: Market
markets.naics_title: Other Food Crops Grown Under Cover
markets.market_name: Electrified Greenhouse Operations with BESS Smoothing
markets.architecture_rationale: Commercial greenhouses (tomato, herb, Dutch model) with grow-light + heat-pump loads. Electrification shifts demand profile to peaks that BESS can smooth. Customer persona is agricultural (not Mein Solar's traditional C&I), different sales channel. Demonstration-project fit only.
